text: This is a symbol of this reactor.
Compared to the last reactor, the key change in this reactor would be we will remove the
middle electrolyte chamber.
There is no splitting of one molecule in the middle chamber in this design.
The overall structure is dual gas configuration, step one.
This is the cathode flow plate.
It also has a splitting flow field.
Also, this is the cathode electrode.
The cathode is also prepared by spray coating copper oxide catalysis onto L2-3-6-3 carbon
paper.
The catalyst loading is also 3 milligram per centimeter square.
Step three, we add this cathode gasket.
The gasket is the silicon gasket.
The thickness is 0.2 micrometer.
And the inner window side is 1 by 1 centimeter square.
So the active area is also 1 centimeter by 1 centimeter.
Step four.
We place the alkaline ion change membrane.
This is the only membrane separator in this reactor.
The membrane is a cell X3-750.
In this design, there is no middle electrolyte chamber, no liquid coagulating, and no solid
resin electrolyte layer.
But the membrane is wetting using the one molecule H.
The purpose is to simplify the reactor structure, remove the liquid middle layer, and avoid
liquid-induced flooding or pressure imbalance.
Step five.
We add the anode gasket.
The anode gasket is also silicon materials and is also 0.2 micrometer.
This is the anode electrode.
The anode is also prepared by spray coating PT-Rosinium carbon catalysis onto H2-3-3 carbon
paper.
The catalyst loading is also 2 milligram per centimeter square.
Because the window of the anode gasket is also 1 by 1, so the active area for anode
is also 1 by 1 centimeter square.
This is the anode flow plate.
It also has a sprinting flow field.
Finally, after aligning all components, we assemble and tighten the reactor using a torque
of 2 newton per newton by meter.
The reactor is also tightened using four bolts in a diagonal sequence for uniform compression.
Using a torque of 2.
Using a torque of...
Okay, so using a torque of 0.3 newton meter.
In summary, this reactor keeps the same cathode and anode configuration as this industrial
reactor, but it removes the middle electrolyte chamber and uses only one AM membrane.
This carries a compact dual-dode gas CRAM H1 reactor designed to reduce flooding risk
and simplify the ion-transparent pathway.
